activities_projects.title: Constitutional Check and Balance Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the power dynamics between the Governor and the Chief Minister
steps: Research the 'Discretionary Powers' of the Governor in the Indian Constitution
Analyze a scenario where the Governor might delay signing a bill passed by the Assembly
Discuss why the Governor is appointed by the Center while the CM is elected by the people
Argue the pros and cons of this 'dual-head' system for state stability
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically examines the federal structure and the balance of power between elected and appointed heads
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of constitutional reasoning and understanding of federalism (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Coalition Politics and Policy Stalemate
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the complexities of governing without a single-party majority
materials: Data on past coalition governments in India
steps: Examine a state election where no party reached the 50% + 1 threshold
Identify the 'Common Minimum Program' (CMP) used to bridge ideological gaps
Simulate a crisis where a smaller partner threatens to withdraw support over a policy
Explain how coalition politics affects the speed of law-making in the Assembly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the impact of multi-party systems on governance and legislative efficiency
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical analysis of the stability and challenges of coalition governments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Legislative vs. Executive Accountability Audit
activities_projects.objective: Trace the path of accountability from a civil servant to the people
materials: Case study on a public health crisis
steps: Map the hierarchy: People $ightarrow$ MLA $ightarrow$ Minister $ightarrow$ Secretary $ightarrow$ District Collector
Analyze who is responsible if a government-run hospital runs out of medicines
Differentiate between 'Political Accountability' (Minister) and 'Administrative Accountability' (Secretary)
Explain the concept of 'Collective Responsibility' in the State Cabinet
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between the permanent executive (bureaucracy) and the political executive
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the accountability chain and conceptual clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Zero Hour' Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the urgency of raising public issues in the House
steps: Assign students roles: Speaker, Opposition MLAs, and Cabinet Ministers
Opposition must raise a 'Matter of Urgent Public Importance' without prior notice
The Minister must provide a spontaneous but factual defense of the department
The Speaker must decide whether to allow the discussion based on 'House Rules'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates the spontaneous and critical nature of legislative oversight
activities_projects.evaluation: Ability to frame urgent questions and provide relevant ministerial responses (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Media as the 'Fourth Estate' Inquiry
activities_projects.objective: Critically analyze media's role in influencing government policy
steps: Trace a news story about a local issue that forced the State Government to take action
Compare how different news channels report on the same Assembly debate
Identify 'bias' or 'sensationalism' that might shift public focus away from real issues
Write an essay on: 'Is the Press Conference a tool for transparency or image management?'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appraises the power of the media to hold the state government accountable
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical analysis of media bias and its impact on public opinion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Budgetary Allocation and Priority Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Understand the fiscal responsibility of the state government
steps: Compare the percentage of funds allocated to 'Education' vs. 'Infrastructure'
Analyze why a government might prioritize one over the other during an election year
Draft a 'Shadow Budget' for your state, proposing changes to improve public welfare
Explain why the state must get permission from the Center to borrow large sums
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes economic data to understand political priorities and constraints
activities_projects.evaluation: Rationality of the shadow budget and use of fiscal data (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Delimitation and Constituency Design
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how boundaries affect democratic representation
steps: Explain the principle of 'Equal Representation' (one person, one vote, one value)
Research why constituencies are redrawn after a census (Delimitation)
Propose a boundary change for a local area to balance the number of voters
Discuss the challenges of representing rural vs. urban interests in a single assembly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the demographic and geographical basis of election management
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical approach to delimitation and population-representation balance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Bill to Law: The Legislative Gauntlet
activities_projects.objective: Trace the rigorous process of passing a state law
materials: Draft of a hypothetical bill (e.g., Plastic Ban Act)
steps: Simulate the 'Three Readings' of a bill in the Legislative Assembly
Include a 'Select Committee' stage where experts are consulted
Identify the point where the Governor can send the bill back for reconsideration
Explain the difference between a 'Money Bill' and an 'Ordinary Bill'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the legal scrutiny and procedures required to create a state law
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct sequencing of the legislative process and committee role (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Inter-State Dispute Resolution Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine how state governments interact with each other and the Center
materials: Cauvery Water Dispute or similar river/border case study
steps: Identify the role of the State Governments involved and their arguments
Analyze why the Central Government or Supreme Court had to intervene
Discuss how 'State Pride' can sometimes conflict with 'National Interest'
Propose a diplomatic solution that respects the sovereignty of both states
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the complexities of inter-state relations in a federal system
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of conflict analysis and fairness of the proposed solution (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Public Interest Litigation (PIL) Workshop
activities_projects.objective: Explore how citizens can use the courts to move the government
steps: Identify a failure of the state government (e.g., pollution in a local river)
Draft a 'Writ Petition' to the High Court under Article 226
Argue how the state's inaction violates the citizens' Fundamental Rights
Explain the concept of 'Judicial Activism' when the government fails to work
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Empowers students to understand legal avenues for seeking governmental accountability
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the legal argument and identification of violated rights (10 marks)
